steps.title: Identify the goal: present Tan's research to an audience unfamiliar with Angkor Wat
steps.content: The notes include: (1) what Angkor Wat is (a Cambodian temple built in the 1100s), (2) what decorrelation stretch analysis is (a digital imaging technique), and (3) what Tan found (hundreds of hidden images). A correct answer must introduce Angkor Wat for an unfamiliar audience AND present the discovery clearly.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Tan photographed Angkor Wat's plaster walls and then applied decorrelation stretch analysis to the photographs.: Describes Tan's method but never identifies what Angkor Wat is; an unfamiliar reader is left without context. ✗
  • Choice B — Decorrelation stretch analysis is a novel digital imaging technique that Tan used to enhance the contrast between colors in a photograph.: Defines the technique but says nothing about where Tan applied it or what he discovered. ✗
  • Choice C — Using a novel digital imaging technique, Tan revealed hundreds of images hidden on the walls of Angkor Wat, a Cambodian temple.: Identifies Angkor Wat as "a Cambodian temple," names the discovery (hundreds of hidden images), and attributes it to Tan — meeting both requirements. ✓
  • Choice D — Built to honor a Hindu god before becoming a Buddhist temple, Cambodia's Angkor Wat concealed hundreds of images on its plaster walls.: Explains Angkor Wat's history but omits Tan's research and discovery entirely. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
